name: Abdominal aortic aneurysm
treatments: lifestyle changes (quit smoking, healthy diet, regular exercise, weight loss, reduce alcohol)
medications to lower blood pressure and cholesterol
elective surgery for large or rapidly growing aneurysm

name: Acanthosis nigricans
symptoms: patches in skin folds (armpits, neck, groin)

name: Acne
treatments: wash affected area twice daily with mild soap and lukewarm water
avoid oil‑based make‑up and skincare products
apply fragrance‑free water‑based emollient if skin is dry
regular exercise and shower promptly after sweating
wash hair regularly and keep it away from face

name: Acoustic neuroma (vestibular schwannoma)
symptoms: facial numbness, pain or weakness (one side)

name: Acromegaly
symptoms: abnormal height growth in children and teenagers (gigantism)
body growth – feet, fingers, head, tongue and neck increase in size
treatments: injections or tablets to reduce growth hormone levels

name: Actinic keratoses (solar keratoses)
symptoms: colour from skin‑tone to pink, red or brown
occurs on sun‑exposed areas (face, hands, arms, ears, scalp, legs)

name: Actinomycosis
symptoms: Lumps in your cheek or neck
pus leaking from the skin on your chest
a lump or swelling in your tummy
pus leaking from the skin on your tummy
Pain low down in your tummy
a lump or swelling in your lower tummy
treatments: oral antibiotic tablets for several months
surgery to drain abscesses and remove infected skin

name: Acute cholecystitis
symptoms: sudden sharp pain in upper right abdomen
tender abdomen, worsened by deep breathing

name: Acute kidney injury (AKI)
treatments: antibiotics or other medicines to treat infection, raise blood pressure, reduce swelling or modulate immune response

name: Acute myeloid leukaemia
symptoms: unusual and frequent bruising or bleeding
treatments: bone marrow or stem cell transplant

name: ADHD in adults
symptoms: difficulty following instructions or completing tasks
treatments: lifestyle modifications (exercise, sleep hygiene, balanced diet)

name: ADHD in children and young people
symptoms: getting up when expected to sit still

name: Albinism
symptoms: very pale skin that burns easily
treatments: low‑vision aids (large‑print materials, magnifiers, telescopic lenses, screen magnification software)
sunglasses, tinted glasses and wide‑brimmed hats for photophobia
surgery for nystagmus or eye muscle correction
glasses, eye exercises, surgery or injections for squint
regular skin checks for skin cancer

name: Alcohol-related liver disease
symptoms: vomiting blood or blood in stools
treatments: support and advice from alcohol addiction services

name: Allergic rhinitis
treatments: salt water nasal spray or rinse

name: Allergies
symptoms: pain or tenderness around cheeks, eyes or forehead
raised rash (hives) or itchy or red skin
swollen eyes, lips, mouth or throat
treatments: steroid nasal sprays, tablets and creams
adrenaline auto‑injectors (e.g., EpiPen) for severe reactions

name: Alzheimer's disease
symptoms: problems moving around without assistance or performing self-care tasks

name: Amyloidosis
treatments: stem cell or bone marrow transplant

name: Anabolic steroid misuse
symptoms: excess growth of facial and body hair
treatments: referral to a specially trained drugs counsellor
gradually reducing the dose of anabolic steroids

name: Anal fissure
symptoms: bright red blood in the toilet or on the toilet paper after pooing
treatments: pain relief medicines like Buscopan or mebeverine
small cut in the muscle in the anus

name: Anal fistula
symptoms: smelly discharge from near the anus
passing pus or blood when pooing
swelling and redness around the anus

name: Androgen insensitivity syndrome
symptoms: lack of pubic and underarm hair

name: Angelman syndrome
symptoms: walking with arms in the air

name: Angioedema
symptoms: swelling of face, lips, tongue, eyelids
skin, tongue or lips turning blue, grey or pale
treatments: identifying and avoiding the underlying cause

name: Animal and human bites
treatments: blood tests for hepatitis B, C and HIV

name: Ankylosing spondylitis
symptoms: pain and swelling in other parts of the body

name: Antiphospholipid syndrome (APS)
symptoms: throbbing pain and swelling in 1 leg
pain and swelling in arm or tummy
chest pain and coughing up blood

name: Appendicitis
symptoms: pain in the middle of the abdomen
pain in the lower right side of the abdomen

name: Ataxia
treatments: medication for muscle, bladder, heart and eye problems

name: Athlete's foot
symptoms: sore and flaky patches on feet

name: Auditory processing disorder (APD)
symptoms: difficulty with strong accents or fast talkers
treatments: wireless earpiece to reduce background noise

name: Autism
symptoms: Restricted and repetitive patterns of behaviour

name: Autosomal recessive polycystic kidney disease
symptoms: problems with blood flow through the liver

name: Bacterial vaginosis
symptoms: unusual vaginal discharge with strong fishy smell
change to colour and consistency of discharge, such as greyish-white and thin and watery

name: Baker's cyst
symptoms: soft lump at the back of the knee
tightness, stiffness or aching in your knee
treatments: draining fluid out of the cyst
surgery to repair damage to your knee joint

name: Bartholin's cyst
symptoms: soft lump on 1 side near the opening of the vagina
discomfort or pain in the area surrounding the vagina
1 side of the vaginal lips looking larger or swollen
feeling unwell, or having a high temperature
treatments: sitting in a bath with warm water
applying a warm cloth or cotton wool to the cyst
taking ibuprofen or paracetamol for pain
surgical procedure to drain the cyst

name: Bedbugs
symptoms: small brown spots on bedding or furniture
treatments: washing and drying bedding and clothing on hot settings

name: Behçet's disease
symptoms: red, painful eyes and blurred vision

name: Bile duct cancer (cholangiocarcinoma)
symptoms: yellowing of the skin and eyes

name: Birthmarks
symptoms: Flat, light or dark brown patches

name: Bladder pain syndrome (BPS)
symptoms: bladder pain or pain in lower tummy or back
feeling like you need to pee, even when bladder is empty
peeing often, usually small amounts, during the day
waking up at night to pee
leaking out a small amount of pee
treatments: everyday painkillers like paracetamol and ibuprofen
painkillers to treat nerve pain like amitriptyline, gabapentin, or pregabalin
medicines that make you pee less like oxybutynin
pentosan polysulfate sodium to protect bladder from irritation
physiotherapy to strengthen pelvic floor muscles

name: Blepharitis
symptoms: flakes or crusts around the roots of the eyelashes
eyelids sticking together in the morning

name: Bone cyst
treatments: cutting or scraping out the cyst

name: Botulism
symptoms: limp or floppy head, neck, arms or legs
treatments: medicine to stop symptoms getting worse

name: Bowel polyps
treatments: surgery to remove part of bowel

name: Costochondritis
symptoms: pain in front or side of chest

name: Bowel incontinence
symptoms: feeling like you need to poo but cannot get to the toilet in time
not being able to get fully clean after going to the toilet
seeing streaks or stains of poo in underwear
treatments: surgery to repair or replace anal sphincter

name: Brain abscess
treatments: draining the pus through a hole in the skull

name: Brain aneurysm
symptoms: pain above or around the eye
numbness or weakness on one side of the face
weakness or numbness in one arm or down one side of the body
blurred vision or loss of sight

name: Breast abscess
symptoms: breast feels warm or looks red
treatments: draining pus with a small cut in the skin

name: Breast cancer in men
symptoms: change in the size or shape of the breast
change in the shape of the nipple

name: Breast cancer in women
symptoms: change in breast size or shape

name: Breath-holding in babies and children
symptoms: cry and then be silent while holding breath
open mouth as if going to cry but make no sound
be floppy or stiff, or body may jerk
treatments: iron supplements if iron levels are low

name: Broken ankle
symptoms: large amount of swelling or bruising
hurts to put weight on it
feels very stiff or is difficult to move

name: Broken collarbone
symptoms: bone sticking out of the skin

name: Broken hip
symptoms: pain in the hip or upper leg
swelling and bruising around your hip and upper leg
not being able to stand or put weight on the affected hip and leg
difficulty moving your hip and leg
the affected leg is at an odd angle or is shorter than the unaffected leg
treatments: surgery to fix the broken bones back into place using screws, nails, rods or plates

name: Broken leg
treatments: surgery with plates, rods or screws
traction to pull bone back into place

name: Broken nose
treatments: hold an ice pack on your nose
stitches or strips for large wounds

name: Broken or bruised ribs
symptoms: swelling or tenderness around affected ribs
treatments: use painkillers like paracetamol or ibuprofen
apply ice pack to affected area
rest and take time off work
breathe normally and cough when needed
hold a pillow against chest when coughing

name: Bronchiectasis
symptoms: cough that does not go away

name: Bronchitis
treatments: antibiotics if caused by bacterial infection

name: Bullous pemphigoid
symptoms: itchy, raised rash on the skin
rash on the backs of the knees, insides of the armpits and elbows, hands, feet or tummy
sore, itchy patches that look red or pink on white skin and dark reddish-brown on brown or black skin
small blisters that may appear after a few weeks
large blisters that can grow quite big and may contain blood
blisters in the mouth that can cause problems swallowing (dysphagia)

name: Bunions
symptoms: hard lumps on the sides of your feet
big toe may point towards your other toes
pain along the side or bottom of your feet
treatments: wide shoes with a low heel and soft sole

name: Burns and scalds
treatments: burn or scald cleaning and dressing

name: Bursitis
symptoms: more painful when moved or pressed

name: Cancer
symptoms: a new lump or swelling anywhere on your body
sweating a lot (particularly at night) or a high temperature that lasts a long time or keeps coming back
unusual bruising or bleeding (such as bleeding from your bottom, blood in your pee or vomiting blood)
pain anywhere in your body that's not usual for you
losing weight without trying or feeling less hungry than usual
needing to pee more often or more urgently, or pain when you pee
a new mole, or changes to a mole anywhere on your body
changes in your skin, such as a rash or sore that does not get better
the whites of your eyes or your skin looking yellow (jaundice) – yellow skin may be harder to see on black or brown skin
heartburn or indigestion that's frequent, very painful or gets worse
a swollen tummy (bloating) that happens often or lasts a long time
changes in your poo, such as needing to poo more or less often than usual, diarrhoea or constipation that does not get better or gets worse
a hoarse or croaky voice that does not get better
a sore or ulcer in your mouth that does not get better
white or red patches in your mouth
a cough that does not get better or gets worse
shortness of breath that does not get better or gets worse
vaginal discharge that's not usual for you
unusual vaginal bleeding, such as bleeding between periods or after sex, or bleeding after the menopause

name: Carbon monoxide poisoning
treatments: tests to check the level of carbon monoxide in your blood

name: Carpal tunnel syndrome
symptoms: ache or pain in fingers, hand or arm
treatments: stop or cut down on things that may be causing it

name: Cataracts in adults
symptoms: eyes being uncomfortable in, or sensitive to, light and glare
looking at 1 object but seeing 2 (double vision)

name: Catarrh
symptoms: feeling like there's a lump or something stuck in your throat
a frequent need to swallow or cough to try to clear your throat
a feeling that mucus is slowly dripping down the back of your throat (postnasal drip)
a crackling feeling in your ears
treatments: sip ice cold water when you need to clear your throat
try using an extra pillow in bed
try rinsing your nose with salt water

name: Cellulitis
symptoms: swelling in fingers or on the back of hands
swelling in feet, sometimes near toes if you have athlete's foot
red, swollen skin around the eye
treatments: raising affected body part on a pillow or chair
regularly moving the joint near the affected body part
not wearing compression stockings until better
keeping skin clean and well moisturised
cleaning any cuts or wounds and using antiseptic cream
preventing cuts and scrapes by wearing appropriate clothing and footwear

name: Cerebral palsy
symptoms: seeming too stiff or too floppy
treatments: medicine for muscle stiffness and other difficulties
surgery to treat movement or growth problems

name: Cervical spondylosis
symptoms: neck and shoulder pain or stiffness that can come and go
headaches that often start at the back of the neck
treatments: take painkillers, like paracetamol and ibuprofen
muscle relaxant or other medicine for chronic pain
surgery for nerve pinching or spinal cord problem

name: Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease
symptoms: muscle weakness in feet, ankles, legs and hands
highly arched or very flat feet
numbness in feet, arms and hands
treatments: physiotherapy and certain types of exercise
surgery to correct problems such as flat feet and muscle contractures

name: Charles Bonnet syndrome
symptoms: patterns such as shapes or lines
in black and white or colour
last for a few minutes or several hours

name: Chest infection
treatments: drink hot lemon and honey drink

name: Chiari malformation
symptoms: numbness or tingling in the arms or legs

name: Childhood cataracts
treatments: Surgery to remove the affected lens

name: Chipped, broken or cracked tooth
symptoms: chipped, cracked or broken a tooth
treatments: gluing the fragment of tooth back on

name: Chlamydia
symptoms: bleeding after sex or between periods
a burning feeling when you pee
white, cloudy or watery discharge from the tip of your penis
burning and itching around the testicles (balls) and penis
pain and swelling in the testicles
a burning feeling when you pee
pain, bleeding, and discharge from your bottom
treatments: tablets you take for 7 to 14 days

name: Cholesteatoma
symptoms: watery, smelly discharge from the ear
gradual loss of hearing in the affected ear
ear infections that keep coming back
treatments: stitches and packing in the ear to stop bleeding

name: Chronic kidney disease
treatments: medicine to help kidneys keep working

name: Chronic myeloid leukaemia
symptoms: Pain in the bones or joints

name: Cirrhosis
symptoms: feeling tired or unwell all the time
loss of appetite or losing weight without trying
visible blood vessels on the skin
blotchy red palms of the hands
yellow skin and whites of the eyes
swollen tummy, legs, ankles or feet
men may also have bigger breasts than usual or their testicles may get smaller
treatments: medicine to make you pee more and help reduce swelling

name: Clostridioides difficile (C. diff) infection
treatments: taking a new 10-day course of another antibiotic

name: Clubfoot
symptoms: foot points inwards, up or down

name: Cluster headaches
symptoms: sharp, burning or piercing pain, usually on 1 side of the head, around the eye
headaches that happen at the same time of year or at set times of the day
headaches that start and stop quickly, without warning
pain that can make you feel restless, you may want to walk around or move your body
headaches that last between 15 minutes and 3 hours
red and watering eye on the same side as the pain
drooping and swelling in the eyelid on the same side as the pain
smaller pupil in the eye on the same side as the pain
treatments: mild electrical pulses to the back of the neck

name: Coeliac disease
symptoms: disorders that affect co-ordination, balance and speech (ataxia)

name: Chronic pancreatitis
symptoms: having very smelly poo that floats and is difficult to flush away
feeling bloated or farting a lot
feeling thirsty or needing to pee a lot
treatments: medicines that help your body absorb nutrients from food
medicines for diabetes, such as insulin
surgery to remove growths (cysts) from your pancreas or unblock blockages in your pancreas or bile ducts

name: Cold sores
symptoms: red spot on face filled with pus
blister on inside of lip or mouth
red sores or blisters on face that become crusty, golden-brown patches
treatments: creams to ease pain and irritation

name: Colic
treatments: sit or hold baby upright during feeding

name: Colour vision deficiency (colour blindness)
symptoms: uses wrong colours when drawing or painting
difficulty with tasks involving sorting colours
treatments: getting treatment for underlying medical condition

name: Common cold
symptoms: feeling of pressure in your ears and face
treatments: drink a hot lemon and honey drink

name: Compartment syndrome
symptoms: swelling or bulging of the muscle
numbness, weakness or pins and needles
tightness or difficulty moving the affected body part

name: Complex regional pain syndrome
symptoms: fluctuating changes in colour or temperature

name: Congenital heart disease
symptoms: being slow in putting on weight and growing
swelling around the tummy and swollen hands, ankles and feet
being sweatier than usual, but skin feels cool and clammy
areas of the skin and lips turning blue or grey (cyanosis)

name: Conjunctivitis
symptoms: produce pus that sticks to lashes
treatments: hold a cold flannel on your eyes

name: Constipation
symptoms: not pooing as often or finding it hard to poo
poo is unusually large or small and is dry, hard or lumpy
straining or in pain when you have a poo
feel like you haven't fully emptied your bowels
treatments: drinking plenty of water and other fluids
resting feet on a low stool while going to the toilet

name: Corns and calluses
symptoms: painful hard or thick areas of skin
treatments: use soft insoles or heel pads
use a pumice stone or foot file
products to treat corns and calluses
specially made soft pads or insoles
cutting away the corn or callus

name: Coronary heart disease
symptoms: pain in your neck, shoulders, jaw or arms

name: Corticobasal degeneration
symptoms: difficulty controlling your limb on one side of the body
treatments: palliative care and advanced care planning

name: Cradle cap
symptoms: white or yellow greasy scales on the scalp and face that form a crust which might flake off
small, dry flakes of skin on the nappy area
skin under and around the scales may look pink or red if your baby has white skin, or lighter or darker than the surrounding skin if your baby has brown or black skin
treatments: massage an emollient or coconut oil on to your baby's scalp to help loosen the scales
gently brush your baby's scalp with a soft brush and then wash it with baby shampoo
bath your baby regularly using water and an emollient instead of soap if scales appear on other areas of the body
change wet or dirty nappies as soon as possible and apply a barrier cream to protect the skin if scales are on the bottom or nappy area

name: Craniosynostosis
symptoms: one side of the head is flattened or bulging out

name: Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease
symptoms: progressive loss of brain function and mobility

name: Crohn's disease
symptoms: blood or mucus in your poo
feeling very tired or generally unwell
loss of appetite and losing weight

name: Croup
symptoms: high pitched, rasping sound when breathing in

name: Cushing's syndrome
symptoms: having more body fat on your neck, upper back, chest and tummy
skin that bruises easily and stretch marks on your tummy, hips, breasts and under your arms
muscle weakness, particularly at the top of your arms and legs
changes in mood, such as feeling irritable, anxious or depressed
irregular periods or periods stopping completely
treatments: gradually reducing how much steroid medicine you take
medicine to control the amount of cortisol your adrenal glands produce
radiotherapy to get rid of a non-cancerous growth in the pituitary gland
surgery to remove a growth in the pituitary gland or adrenal glands
surgery to remove your adrenal glands

name: Cystic fibrosis
symptoms: cough that does not go away and brings up mucus
frequent sinus infections (sinusitis) and chest infections
large, greasy and sticky poos that are hard to flush away
in children, not growing as quickly as expected
in adults, losing weight without trying to
very salty sweat, which can leave small crystals on your skin
treatments: medicines to widen your airways and make breathing easier
steroid medicine to reduce inflammation in the airways

name: Cytomegalovirus (CMV)
symptoms: yellowing of the skin and the whites of the eyes (jaundice)
hearing problems in 1 or both ears
problems with the liver and spleen

name: Dandruff
symptoms: white or grey flakes of skin on the scalp and in the hair
scaly patches on skin on scalp, face and other areas of the body
red or silver rash on scalp, sometimes with patchy hair loss
dry, red, flaky and very itchy skin on areas of the body
red, irritated skin, may also have blisters and cracked skin
red, flaky, crusty and sore patches of skin covered with silvery scales
greasy, yellowish crusts on baby's scalp, eyebrows and nappy area

name: Deafblindness
symptoms: needing to turn up the volume on the television or radio
not hearing noises such as a knock at the door
asking others to speak loudly, slowly and more clearly
needing to hold books or newspapers very close, or sitting close to the television
treatments: using a hearing aid or implant
training the person to use a long cane, a guide dog or offering a communicator guide

name: Dehydration
symptoms: a dry mouth, lips and tongue

name: Dementia with Lewy bodies
symptoms: problems with understanding, thinking, memory and judgement
slow movement, stiff limbs and tremors
disturbed sleep, often with violent movements and shouting out
treatments: medicines to reduce hallucinations, confusion, drowsiness, movement problems and disturbed sleep
physiotherapy, occupational therapy and speech and language therapy
psychological therapies, such as cognitive stimulation
activities for dementia, such as memory cafes

name: Dengue
symptoms: blotchy rash made up of flat or slightly raised spots
blood in your vomit or poo
treatments: taking paracetamol to help bring down your temperature and ease any pain

name: Detached retina (retinal detachment)
symptoms: floaters (dots and lines) or flashes of light in your eye
a dark 'curtain' or shadow in your vision
changes to your eyesight, such as blurred vision
treatments: surgery (vitrectomy, scleral buckling, pneumatic retinopexy, cryotherapy)

name: Developmental co-ordination disorder (dyspraxia) in children
treatments: breaking down difficult movements into smaller parts
adapting tasks to make them easier
using special grips on pens and pencils

name: Developmental dysplasia of the hip
symptoms: osteoarthritis of the hip and back
femur coming out of the socket (dislocate)
one leg cannot be moved out sideways as far as the other
one leg seems to be longer than the other
one leg drags when they crawl
treatments: surgery (reduction, closed reduction, open reduction, bone surgery (osteotomy))

name: Diabetes insipidus
symptoms: peeing a lot, even at night
need to pee small amounts at frequent intervals
pee more than 10 times a day

name: Diabetic ketoacidosis
treatments: nutrients through a drip into your vein

name: Dental abscess
symptoms: intense toothache or pain in your gums
redness inside the mouth, or outside the mouth on the face or jaw
sensitivity to hot or cold food and drink in the affected area
a bad taste in your mouth
difficulty opening your mouth and chewing food
swollen glands on each side of your neck
treatments: call 999 or go to A&E if you have difficulty breathing, speaking or swallowing, or have a swollen or painful eye

name: Diphtheria
symptoms: high pitched, rasping sound when breathing in

name: Discoid eczema
symptoms: dry, cracked, crusty, scaly or thickened skin

name: Dislocated kneecap
symptoms: cannot stand up or move knee
felt a pop or snap in knee
treatments: kneecap may need to be put back into place by a doctor

name: Diverticular disease and diverticulitis
symptoms: pain in the lower left side of your tummy (abdomen)
tummy pain that gets worse after you eat, and gets better after you poo or fart
bleeding or passing slime (mucus) from your bottom
treatments: bulk-forming laxatives for constipation and diarrhoea
medicines to help with stomach cramps (antispasmodics)
antibiotics if the diverticulitis is caused by an infection

name: DVT (deep vein thrombosis)
symptoms: throbbing pain and swelling in 1 leg
pain and swelling in your arm or tummy
warm, red or darkened skin around the painful or swollen area
swollen veins that are hard or sore when you touch them
bleeding more easily than other people
a reddish-blue, lace-like rash on your lower legs
treatments: draining fluid out of the cyst
surgery to repair damage to your knee joint

name: Dysarthria (difficulty speaking)
symptoms: difficulty moving your mouth, tongue or lips
difficulty controlling the volume of your voice
speaking in short bursts instead of full sentences
treatments: exercises to strengthen the muscles used for speech
strategies to make your speech easier to understand

name: Dyslexia in adults
symptoms: need to read something several times before it makes sense
feel tired if you do a lot of reading and writing
find it difficult to tell left from right
find it hard to plan things or manage your time
forget things like dates or people's names
get confused if you're asked to follow more than one instruction at a time
may be skilled in some areas but struggle with other tasks most people find easy to complete
treatments: wear a wireless earpiece that connects to a tiny microphone worn by your teacher

name: Dupuytren's contracture
symptoms: dimples or ridges on the palm
fingers bend in towards the palm
fingers get stuck in a bent position
fingers cannot be put down flat
treatments: surgery to remove the affected tissue in the hand (fasciectomy)
using a needle to make small cuts in the affected tissue (needle fasciotomy)
surgery to remove the affected tissue and skin (dermofasciectomy)

name: Dyslexia in children
symptoms: learning to speak later than other children
not being interested in activities around language, like saying the alphabet
reading at a level below what's expected at their age
having difficulty with numbers, telling the time, and remembering the order of things, like days of the week
written work that looks messy and has lots of spelling mistakes
being slow and hesitant when reading aloud
finding it challenging to plan and organise schoolwork
treatments: support from the British Dyslexia Association

name: Dystonia
symptoms: parts of your body twisting into unusual positions

name: Ear infections
symptoms: watery, smelly, discharge that comes out of the ear
gradual loss of hearing in the affected ear

name: Early or delayed puberty
symptoms: thinning hair and hair loss from the head

name: Earwax build-up
symptoms: watery, smelly, discharge that comes out of the ear
gradual loss of hearing in the affected ear
ear infections that keep coming back
treatments: surgery in hospital to remove a cholesteatoma

name: Ebola virus disease
symptoms: being very sleepy or difficult to wake
stiff body or be floppy or unresponsive
bulging soft spot on the top of their head

name: Ectopic pregnancy
symptoms: pain and bloating in your tummy (abdomen)
treatments: call 111 or get help from 111 online

name: Ectropion
symptoms: produce pus that sticks to lashes
treatments: wash your eyelids with clean water
clean your eyelids twice a day
continue to clean your eyes, even if your symptoms clear up

name: Edwards' syndrome (trisomy 18)
symptoms: tendency to stick the tongue out
skin, hair and eyes that are paler than other family members
a wide mouth with widely spaced teeth

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndromes
symptoms: pain and stiffness in joints or muscles

name: Ejaculation problems
symptoms: swelling that later causes a hard lump to develop on the shaft of your penis and stops it stretching during erections
a curve in your penis when it's erect
painful erections that can make having sex difficult
problems getting or keeping an erection
other changes to your penis – for example, you may have a hard area on the top, bottom and side of your penis, shortening it and making it narrower in the middle
treatments: treatments to ease the symptoms and stop it getting worse

name: Encephalitis
symptoms: being very sleepy or difficult to wake
stiff body or be floppy or unresponsive
bulging soft spot on the top of their head

name: Endocarditis
symptoms: being slow in putting on weight and growing
swelling around the tummy and swollen hands, ankles and feet
being sweatier than usual, but skin feels cool and clammy
areas of the skin and lips turning blue or grey
limp, floppy or not responding like they normally do

name: Endometriosis
symptoms: bloating, heaviness or fullness in your tummy

name: Enlarged prostate
symptoms: bladder pain or pain in your lower tummy or back
feeling like you need to pee, even when your bladder is empty
peeing often, usually just small amounts, during the day
waking up at night to pee
leaking out a small amount of pee if you do not get to a toilet quickly enough
pain during sex - women can find sex (penetration) painful and men can find getting an erection painful
pain or burning when you pee
peeing more often then usual, including at night
having difficulty peeing or not being able to pee at all
peeing more than usual, having a sudden urge to pee or getting up often in the night to pee
treatments: tests on a sample of your pee
scans such as an ultrasound or CT scan

name: Epididymitis
symptoms: swollen and sore head of the penis

name: Epiglottitis
symptoms: coughing or choking when eating or drinking
bringing food back up, sometimes through the nose
a feeling that food is stuck in your throat or chest
a gurgly, wet-sounding voice when eating or drinking

name: Epilepsy
symptoms: Increased risk of seizure during labour and after birth

name: Erectile dysfunction (impotence)
symptoms: swelling that later causes a hard lump to develop on the shaft of your penis and stops it stretching during erections
a curve in your penis when it's erect
painful erections that can make having sex difficult
problems getting or keeping an erection
treatments: treatments to stop it getting worse

name: Erythema multiforme
symptoms: rash inside mouth or on lips, eyes, anus or genitals

name: Erythema nodosum
symptoms: large areas of skin are red and tender or painful

name: Eye cancer
symptoms: a new lump or swelling in the eye
changes in the skin around the eye
a sore or ulcer in the eye

name: Eye injuries
symptoms: Bruising and swelling around the eye
Warm or leaking pus around the eye

name: Falls
symptoms: finding it hard to carry out familiar daily tasks
struggling to follow a conversation or find the right word
being confused about time and place
increasing difficulties with tasks and activities that require organisation and planning
treatments: bisphosphonates (alendronic acid, ibandronic acid, risedronate, zoledronic acid)

name: Febrile seizures
symptoms: being very sleepy or difficult to wake
stiff body or be floppy or unresponsive
bulging soft spot on the top of their head
treatments: use NHS 111 online or call NHS 111 for advice

name: Fibroids
symptoms: bloating, heaviness or fullness in your tummy

name: Fibromyalgia
treatments: Available to ease some of the symptoms

name: Flat feet
symptoms: No gap (arch) under your foot when you stand up, your foot presses flat on the floor
treatments: rest and raise your foot when you can
put an ice pack (or bag of frozen peas in a towel) on the painful area for up to 20 minutes every 2 to 3 hours
wear shoes with plenty of room for your feet that have a low heel
wear shoes with cushioned heels and good arch support
use insoles or heel pads in your shoes
try regular gentle exercises to stretch the sole of your foot and your heel
try exercises that do not put pressure on your feet, such as swimming
take painkillers like paracetamol or ibuprofen gel, or ibuprofen tablets if you need them
try to lose weight if you're overweight

name: Flat head syndrome (plagiocephaly and brachycephaly)
symptoms: head is long and narrow, like a rugby ball
one side of their head is flattened or bulging out
soft spot on the top of their head (fontanelle) disappears before they're 1 year old
breathing problems, like not being able to breathe through their mouth
dental problems, such as missing or crowded teeth
speech problems or they're slow to start talking
difficulty breathing normally through their mouth, which can lead to snoring and sleep problems at night

name: Food allergy
symptoms: pain or tenderness around your cheeks, eyes or forehead
swollen eyes, lips, mouth or throat
treatments: Administer epinephrine (adrenaline) via an EpiPen or other auto-injector

name: Food intolerance
symptoms: pain or tenderness around your cheeks, eyes or forehead
swollen eyes, lips, mouth or throat
treatments: salt water nasal sprays or solutions

name: Food poisoning
symptoms: having a limp or floppy head, neck, arms or legs
treatments: medicine to stop symptoms getting worse
helping body do things until recovery

name: Foot drop
symptoms: pins and needles in the affected body part
numbness and less ability to feel pain or changes in temperature, particularly in your feet
a burning or sharp pain, usually in the feet
feeling pain from something that should not be painful at all, such as a very light touch
loss of balance or co-ordination caused by less ability to tell the position of the feet or hands
difficulty lifting up the front part of your foot and toes, particularly noticeable when walking
treatments: treating any underlying cause or symptoms
stopping the medicine that is causing peripheral neuropathy
medicine to treat nerve pain (neuropathic pain)

name: Frontotemporal dementia
symptoms: finding it hard to carry out familiar daily tasks
struggling to follow a conversation or find the right word
being confused about time and place

name: Frozen shoulder
symptoms: pain at the top and the outside of your shoulder
shoulder pain that's worse when you lift your arm
treatments: exercises from your GP or physiotherapist
surgery to widen the space around the tendon

name: Fungal nail infection
symptoms: sore and flaky patches on feet
cracked or bleeding skin on feet

name: Gallbladder cancer
symptoms: sudden, sharp pain in the upper right-hand side of your tummy (abdomen)
yellowing of the skin and the whites of the eyes (jaundice)

name: Gallstones
symptoms: sudden, sharp pain in the upper right-hand side of your tummy (abdomen)
persistent pain that does not go away within a few hours
yellowing of the skin and the whites of the eyes (jaundice)

name: Ganglion cyst
treatments: draining the fluid with a needle and injecting medicine into the bone
cutting or scraping out the cyst

name: Gender dysphoria
symptoms: Feeling of being trapped in wrong body

name: Genital herpes
symptoms: small, rough lumps on the skin

name: Genital warts
symptoms: small, rough lumps on the skin
itching or bleeding from the genitals or anus
a change to the normal flow of pee
painless lumps or growths around the vagina, penis or anus

name: Gestational diabetes
symptoms: needing to pee more often than usual

name: Giardiasis
symptoms: loss of appetite and weight loss

name: Gilbert's syndrome
symptoms: visible blood vessels on the skin
swollen tummy, legs, ankles or feet

name: Glandular fever
treatments: drink a hot lemon and honey drink

name: Glaucoma
symptoms: eyes being uncomfortable in, or sensitive to, light and glare
looking at 1 object but seeing 2 (double vision)

name: Glue ear
treatments: stitches and packing in the ear

name: Goitre
treatments: surgery to remove the thyroid gland

name: Gout
symptoms: sudden severe pain in a joint
hot, swollen, red skin over the affected joint
feeling sick or unable to eat

name: Guillain-Barré syndrome
symptoms: pins and needles in the affected body part
numbness and less ability to feel pain or changes in temperature
a burning or sharp pain, usually in the feet
feeling pain from something that should not be painful at all
difficulty lifting up the front part of your foot and toes
treatments: stopping a medicine that may be causing peripheral neuropathy
medicine to treat nerve pain (neuropathic pain)

name: Gum disease
symptoms: intense toothache or pain in your gums
redness inside the mouth, or outside the mouth on the face or jaw
sensitivity to hot or cold food and drink in the affected area
difficulty opening your mouth and chewing food
swollen glands on each side of your neck

name: Haemochromatosis
symptoms: visible blood vessels on the skin
yellow skin and whites of the eyes
swollen tummy, legs, ankles or feet

name: Haemolytic disease of the fetus and newborn (HDFN)
symptoms: skin or whites of the eyes turn yellow
dark yellow urine or pale-coloured poo
being sleepy, and not wanting to feed or not feeding as well as usual
treatments: weekly immunoglobin treatments for you, to prevent your baby from getting anaemia
light therapy on your baby's skin for jaundice

name: Haemophilia
symptoms: pain when looking at a bright light
treatments: call 999 and ask for an ambulance

name: Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib)
symptoms: sleepier than normal or difficult to wake
blue, grey, pale or blotchy skin, lips or tongue
rash that looks like small bruises or bleeding under the skin

name: Hamstring injury
symptoms: sharp pain in the back of the leg

name: Hand, foot and mouth disease
symptoms: raised rash of spots on the hands and feet
treatments: using mouth ulcer gels, sprays and mouthwashes

name: Hay fever
symptoms: itchy throat, mouth, nose and ears
pain around the sides of your head and your forehead
treatments: pollen filter in car air vents

name: Head injury and concussion
symptoms: clear fluid from ears or nose

name: Head lice and nits
treatments: combing out with a fine-tooth comb

name: Fetal alcohol spectrum disorder
symptoms: movement, balance, vision and hearing problems
learning problems, such as thinking, concentration, and memory issues
managing emotions and developing social skills difficulties
communication problems, such as speech difficulties
joint, muscle, bone, and organ problems, such as kidney and heart issues

name: Gonorrhoea
symptoms: yellow or greenish discharge from your vagina
fluid or discharge coming out of your penis
pain, itching and discharge from your anus

name: Growing pains
symptoms: aching or throbbing in both legs
leg pain when walking or taking part in activities
leg pain that's bad enough to stop them walking or makes them limp
pain in a joint, such as their knees or ankles
rash, swelling or unusual bruising on the legs
leg pain and a high temperature
feels unusually tired or sleepy a lot of the time
does not want to eat or is losing weight
treatments: put a covered hot water bottle or heat pack on the painful area
give children's ibuprofen or paracetamol to ease the pain

name: Hearing loss
symptoms: difficulty hearing other people clearly and misunderstanding what they say, especially in noisy places
needing to ask people to repeat themselves
listening to music or watching TV with the volume higher than other people need
finding it hard to keep up with a conversation
feeling tired or stressed from having to concentrate while listening

name: Heart attack
symptoms: chest pain which may feel like crushing or squeezing on your chest

name: Heart failure
symptoms: being slow in putting on weight and growing
swelling around the tummy and swollen hands, ankles and feet
being sweatier than usual, but skin feels cool and clammy
areas of the skin and lips turning blue or grey (cyanosis)
heart rhythm problems, like the heart beating too quickly, or without a steady beat
treatments: an injection of alcohol into their heart
a pacemaker to regulate the heart rate
an implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) to prevent a life-threatening abnormal heart rhythm

name: Heart rhythm problems (arrhythmia)
symptoms: irregular, with skipped or extra beats (ectopic beats)
treatments: a procedure to help control your heartbeat

name: Heartburn and acid reflux
treatments: try to lose weight if overweight
try to find ways to relax
avoid food and drink that triggers symptoms
avoid eating within 3 or 4 hours before bed

name: Heat exhaustion and heatstroke
symptoms: skin becoming pale and clammy or getting a heat rash
cramps in the arms, legs and stomach
treatments: drink a sports or rehydration drink, or cool water
cool their skin with cool water and a fan
use cold packs under the armpits or on the neck

name: Heat rash (prickly heat)
treatments: drink plenty of fluid to avoid dehydration

name: Henoch-Schönlein purpura (HSP)
symptoms: rash with raised red or purple spots

name: Hepatitis
symptoms: yellowing of your skin and the whites of your eyes (jaundice)
pain in your upper tummy, usually under the right side of your ribs
feeling or being sick (nausea or vomiting)
treatments: medicine to help calm your immune system
lifestyle changes like drinking less alcohol or keeping to a healthy weight

name: Hepatitis A
symptoms: yellowing of your skin and the whites of your eyes (jaundice)
pain in your upper tummy, usually under the right side of your ribs
feeling or being sick (nausea or vomiting)

name: Hepatitis B
symptoms: yellowing of your skin and the whites of your eyes (jaundice)
feeling or being sick (nausea or vomiting)
a raised rash that may be itchy (hives)
yellowing of the skin and whites of the eyes (jaundice)
treatments: Use a condom or dam when having vaginal, anal or oral sex
Avoid sharing razors, toothbrushes and needles with others

name: Hepatitis C
symptoms: yellowing of the skin or the whites of the eyes (jaundice)

name: Hereditary haemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT)
symptoms: neurological problems, such as dizziness, slurred speech (dysarthria), double vision, balance problems and tremor
weakness, numbness, tiredness, memory problems and difficulty concentrating
a type of stroke called a haemorrhagic stroke
visible red spots in certain places on the body

name: Hereditary spastic paraplegia
symptoms: pins and needles in the affected body part
numbness and less ability to feel pain or changes in temperature
a burning or sharp pain, usually in the feet
feeling pain from something that should not be painful at all
difficulty lifting up the front part of your foot and toes
treatments: stopping the medicine that's causing peripheral neuropathy
medicine to treat nerve pain (neuropathic pain)

name: Hernia
symptoms: swelling or lump in the tummy (abdomen) or groin
lump can often be pushed back in or disappears when lying down
coughing or straining may make the lump appear

name: Herpes simplex eye infections
symptoms: blurred vision or other changes to your eyesight
blisters or a rash on your eyelid or the skin around your eye

name: Herpetic whitlow (whitlow finger)
symptoms: swelling and pain in your finger
blisters or sores on your finger
skin becoming red or darker than your usual skin tone
feeling generally unwell and having a high temperature
treatments: keep the affected finger clean and covered with a dressing
take painkillers, such as paracetamol or ibuprofen, to ease the pain
try to discourage children with a whitlow from sucking their thumb

name: Hiatus hernia
symptoms: painful burning feeling in your chest, often after eating (heartburn)
bringing up small amounts of food or bitter-tasting fluids (acid reflux)
treatments: raise the head end of your bed by 10 to 20cm
try to lose weight if you're overweight
try to find ways to relax
take antacids or alginates with food or soon after eating

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS)
symptoms: channels in the skin that leak pus
fleshy lumps on the surface of the skin
sinus tracts under the skin that break out on the surface and leak pus

name: High blood sugar (hyperglycaemia)
treatments: eating or drinking something that will raise your blood sugar quickly
checking your blood sugar after 10 to 15 minutes
having another sugary drink or snack, and checking again after 10 minutes
eating something that will keep your blood sugar up for longer

name: Hirschsprung's disease
symptoms: failing to pass meconium within 48 hours
not feeding well or not gaining much weight

name: Hives
symptoms: pink or red rash on white skin
harder to see on brown and black skin

name: Human papillomavirus (HPV)
symptoms: painless growths or lumps around the vagina, penis or anus
treatments: treatment for genital warts or changes to cells in the cervix

name: Huntington's disease
symptoms: changes in your behaviour and personality
becoming clumsy and finding it difficult to control your muscles
your muscles becoming stiff, and movement becoming slower and more difficult
treatments: medicines to help with mood and behaviour changes
medicines to help with symptoms such as twitching and jerking (chorea)
physiotherapy to help with movement problems
occupational therapy to help with daily activities

name: Hydronephrosis
symptoms: pain in the lower back, side or tummy (abdomen)
needing to pee more often than usual
a high temperature, or feeling hot, cold or shivery
treatments: blood or pee tests to check for infections
an X-ray, CT scan or other tests that take pictures of your body

name: Hypoparathyroidism
symptoms: tingling or burning sensation in fingers, toes and face
brittle nails, dry hair and dry skin
treatments: dietary changes (eating foods high in calcium, avoiding red meat and starchy foods)

name: Hypothermia
symptoms: unusually quiet and sleepy and may refuse to feed
treatments: move the person indoors or somewhere sheltered as quickly as possible
remove any wet clothing, wrap them in a blanket, sleeping bag or dry towel, making sure their head is covered
give them a warm non-alcoholic drink and some sugary food like chocolate if they're fully awake
keep them awake by talking to them until help arrives
monitor heart rate and give oxygen to help breathe
give warm fluids straight into a vein to help body warm up
treatment in intensive care may be needed if severe

name: Ichthyosis
symptoms: dry, itchy, sore, flaky patches of skin
small brown and yellow spots on the palms of your hands and soles of your feet

name: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
symptoms: loss of appetite and weight loss
treatments: exercises and advice to help you breathe more easily (pulmonary rehabilitation)

name: Indigestion
symptoms: bringing up food or bitter-tasting fluids into your mouth
treatments: cut down on tea, coffee, cola or alcohol
raise your head and shoulders up when in bed

name: Infertility
treatments: medical treatment for lack of regular ovulation

name: Inflammatory bowel disease
symptoms: diarrhoea that lasts longer than 4 weeks
blood or mucus in your poo

name: Ingrown hairs
symptoms: Raised, itchy bumps on the skin

name: Ingrown toenail
treatments: wearing wide, comfortable shoes or sandals

name: Insect bites and stings
symptoms: pain where you were bitten or stung
a small, swollen lump on the skin
a mark on your skin where you were bitten or stung
itchy and raised skin around the bite or sting
lots of bites grouped together in a small area
skin around the bite feels hot when you touch it or looks red
pus or fluid coming out of the bite or sting
a round or oval rash that may look like a bullseye
tummy pain and are being sick
stung in your mouth or throat, or near your eyes

name: Japanese encephalitis
symptoms: being very sleepy or difficult to wake
stiff body or be floppy or unresponsive
bulging soft spot on the top of their head

name: Jaundice
symptoms: yellow under tongue or inside cheeks

name: Jaundice in babies
symptoms: yellow in the whites of their eyes
yellow under their tongue or inside their cheeks
pee is dark yellow or brown
poo is a pale creamy colour
treatments: encouraging your baby to feed regularly

name: Jellyfish and other sea creature stings
symptoms: Painful redness and swelling at the sting site
Numbness or tingling around the mouth or face
Blisters or welts at the sting site
Itching or burning sensation at the sting site
treatments: Rinse the affected area with salt water
Remove any remaining stingers or spines
Apply a topical pain reliever such as lidocaine or benzocaine
Take an oral antihistamine such as diphenhydramine to reduce itching and swelling

name: Jet lag
symptoms: Feeling sleepy or tired in the daytime

name: Joint hypermobility syndrome
symptoms: often get tired, even after rest
keep getting pain and stiffness in your joints or muscles

name: Kawasaki disease
symptoms: being very sleepy or difficult to wake
stiff body or be floppy or unresponsive
bulging soft spot on the top of their head

name: Intracranial hypertension
symptoms: finding it difficult or painful to look at bright lights
hearing a pulsing rhythmic noise in your ears

name: Iron deficiency anaemia
symptoms: hearing ringing, buzzing or hissing noises inside your head (tinnitus)
wanting to eat non-food items, such as paper or ice (pica)
painful open sores (ulcers) in the corners of your mouth
nail changes, such as spoon-shaped nails

name: Keratosis pilaris
symptoms: Small, painless bumps on your skin
Patches of bumps that are the same colour as your skin

name: Kidney infection
symptoms: pain in your lower back or side
pain, burning or stinging when you pee
needing to pee more often and urgently than usual
treatments: take paracetamol to reduce a high temperature and relieve pain
give your child liquid paracetamol for children if they're distressed or uncomfortable

name: Kidney stones
symptoms: pain or burning when you pee
peeing more often then usual, including at night
having difficulty peeing or not being able to pee at all
treatments: tests on a sample of your pee
scans such as an ultrasound or CT scan

name: Knocked-out tooth
treatments: gluing the fragment of tooth back on

name: Kyphosis
symptoms: the ribs sticking out on 1 side

name: Labyrinthitis and vestibular neuritis
symptoms: hearing sounds that come from inside the body (tinnitus)
the sensation that you're moving or spinning (vertigo)
numbness, pain or weakness on 1 side of the face
problems with limb co-ordination (ataxia) on 1 side of the body
treatments: treating any underlying cause or symptoms
medicine to treat any nerve pain (neuropathic pain)

name: Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome
symptoms: difficulty lifting up the front part of your foot and toes

name: Laryngitis
symptoms: feeling of pressure in your ears and face
treatments: drink a hot lemon and honey drink
